comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
Correction: the previous CLI/terminal-support comment on this bead was corrupted by shell interpolation of backticked command names. Treat this comment as authoritative.

CLI and terminal support decision:

- `yzx`, `yzx help`, `yzx -h`, and `yzx --help` show help.
- `yzx enter` enters Yazelix inside the current terminal. This is the terminal-agnostic path for Kitty, Alacritty, Foot, Ghostty, WezTerm, and other user-preferred terminals.
- `yzx launch` launches the curated Mars Yazelix app. The desktop entry should call this.
- `yzx attach` is reserved for a future explicit attach/session picker.

Mental model:

- `launch` = bring your own Yazelix window.
- `enter` = bring your own terminal.
- `attach` = bring your own existing session.

Terminal support policy:

Ship Mars as the owned/default experience, but support mainstream terminals through documentation that teaches users to configure their terminal's initial command to `yzx enter`. Yazelix Next should not own Kitty/Alacritty/Foot/Ghostty/WezTerm native configs, themes, fonts, opacity, cursor effects, or per-terminal generated materialization. Once the terminal starts `yzx enter`, Yazelix owns the managed runtime.

Implementation implication:

This bead should treat `yzx enter` as the first important session command. It must attach/create predictably and preserve Yazi-to-Helix bridge isolation. `yzx launch` can wrap Mars around the same runtime entry path. `yzx attach` should remain future scope unless the user explicitly pulls it in.

title: Add bounded power-user Yazi configuration
description:
## Why

Concrete power-user reports now justify expanding the intentionally narrow Yazi surface. Users need native `yazi.toml` settings such as `mgr.ratio`, `show_hidden`, preview configuration, fetchers/previewers, plugins, and flavors. The existing append-only sidecars solve Lua/keymap extension but cannot express those workflows.

Main Yazelix demonstrates both the value and the risk: broad generated configuration, imports, migrations, and ambiguous array merging created a large ownership surface and regressions. Yazelix Next should accept the one-time implementation cost of a bounded native materializer without recreating that system.

## Evidence

- `runtime/yzn-yazi.rs:32-39`: runtime materialization activates only for managed `init.lua` or `keymap.toml`.
- `runtime/yzn-yazi.rs:76-110`: the launcher currently owns config-home materialization inline.
- `runtime/yzn-yazi.rs:86-87`: runtime `yazi.toml` is always the packaged file.
- `runtime/yzn-yazi.rs:107-109`: managed plugins are overlaid only when user `init.lua` exists.
- `home-manager/module.nix:69-75`: Home Manager exposes only Yazi init and keymap files.
- https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/363 and https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/593 show real native-TOML and array-replacement needs.
- https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/594 and https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/358 show real plugin and flavor needs.

## Outcome

- One local Rust crate owns deterministic Yazi config-home materialization.
- Power users can use a managed native Yazi tree under `~/.config/yazelix-next/yazi/`.
- Yazelix keeps only the integration nodes required for the managed sidebar/editor contract.
- Generated runtime state remains disposable and atomic.
- The implementation stays local until a concrete second consumer justifies extraction.

## Scope

In scope:
- Structured managed `yazi.toml` layering.
- Existing `init.lua`, `keymap.toml`, and plugin behavior moved under the single materializer owner.
- Managed plugins, flavors, and narrowly justified package metadata.
- Focused power-user acceptance validation.
- A later extraction decision with explicit second-consumer criteria.

Out of scope:
- Automatic reads or imports from ambient `~/.config/yazi`.
- Migration or legacy compatibility.
- Theme/plugin catalogs or package installation.
- Arbitrary Yazi binary selection.
- Ratconfig fields for native Yazi settings.
- Main Yazelix parity or mechanical porting.

## Verification

Each implementation child must run its focused Cargo tests and the exact changed-surface Nix checks. Runtime children must also pass:

- `nix flake check`
- `nix flake show --all-systems`
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs`
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>`
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next`

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if implementation begins validating the full Yazi schema.
- Stop if import, migrations, backup policy, or compatibility aliases become required.
- Stop if more than a small explicit set of Yazelix-owned TOML nodes is needed.
- Stop if a separate repository is proposed without a concrete second consumer that will delete its existing owner.

## Maintenance Notes

This epic reactivates the earlier design guardrail because concrete user evidence now exists. Previously completed low-LOC init, keymap, and plugin-overlay beads remain the behavioral baseline. Rejected: porting main Yazelix materialization or extracting a repository before the local contract is proven.
notes: Prime-beads review 2026-06-27: this bead is a useful design record, but not ready implementation work without a concrete user-visible Yazi workflow that packaged defaults cannot satisfy. Reactivate by opening or updating a focused implementation bead when that workflow exists; keep broad Yazi config materialization rejected by default.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Decision update 2026-06-28: keep this broad Yazi config-management bead deferred as the guardrail. The low-LOC path is now split into focused implementation beads: yazelix-next-yazi-init-lua-extension-qxx for optional managed init.lua layering, yazelix-next-ratconfig-yazi-init-sidecar-4pm for Ratconfig discovery after runtime support exists, and yazelix-next-yazi-plugin-dir-overlay-t6c for manual plugin directory overlay only after the generated config-home owner exists. Full main parity, import, themes/flavors, package.toml, and TOML/keymap merging remain rejected until concrete user pressure justifies them.

title: Add local Yazi materializer with native yazi.toml layering
description:
## Why

Power users cannot currently apply native Yazi settings such as `mgr.ratio`, `show_hidden`, preview limits, or custom fetcher/previewer tables because managed Yazi always uses the packaged `yazi.toml`. The materialization logic has also outgrown its inline launcher ownership: adding structured TOML safely requires Cargo dependencies and isolated tests.

## User-Visible Contract

An optional `~/.config/yazelix-next/yazi/yazi.toml` extends the packaged Yazi configuration. With no user file, current behavior and the immutable packaged config fast path remain unchanged.

Merge rules are intentionally small and documented:

- TOML tables merge recursively.
- User scalars replace packaged scalars.
- User arrays replace packaged arrays by default; generic array concatenation is forbidden.
- Yazelix reasserts the managed `opener.edit` route after merging so file opens still use `yzn-open`.
- The two packaged Git fetchers required for sidebar decorations remain present exactly once. User fetchers/previewers survive according to one explicit named policy; do not generalize that exception to arbitrary arrays.
- Invalid user TOML fails before Yazi starts with the source path and a concise diagnostic.

## Evidence

- `runtime/yzn-yazi.rs:30-73`: `yzn-yazi` owns launch environment and final exec.
- `runtime/yzn-yazi.rs:76-110`: config materialization is inline in the launcher.
- `runtime/yzn-yazi.rs:86-87`: `yazi.toml` is always symlinked from the package.
- `yazi/yazi.toml:1-14`: the packaged file currently owns Git fetchers and the managed edit opener.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/file_actions.rs:47-130`: Advanced native file actions already expose managed Yazi files without parsing them.
- `home-manager/module.nix:69-75`: declarative Yazi ownership lacks `yazi.toml`.
- https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/363 demonstrates table/array-of-table layering needs.
- https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/593 demonstrates why `mgr.ratio` arrays must replace rather than append.

## Scope

In scope:
- Create a local Cargo crate such as `crates/yzn-yazi-config` as the sole config-home materialization owner.
- Move the current `init.lua`, `keymap.toml`, and plugin materialization behavior into the crate without changing those contracts.
- Keep `runtime/yzn-yazi.rs` as the Yazi process/env launcher and delegate materialization to the crate.
- Add structured optional `yazi.toml` layering with the rules above.
- Write generated state atomically below `YAZELIX_STATE_DIR/yazi`.
- Activate materialization when any supported managed Yazi input exists, not only init/keymap.
- Add a path-constrained Advanced action for managed `yazi/yazi.toml`; Ratconfig must not parse native Yazi settings.
- Add `programs.yazelix.config.yazi.config` text/source passthrough to Home Manager.
- Preserve the existing managed Yazi opener, sidebar state, Alt-z behavior, and packaged plugin collision policy.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and the LOC scorecard.
- Refresh the installed runtime after the flake change.

Out of scope:
- Do not import `~/.config/yazi`.
- Do not add migrations, legacy paths, backups, or reset behavior.
- Do not expose Yazi fields in root semantic config or Ratconfig.
- Do not manage plugins, flavors, or `package.toml` beyond preserving the current plugin behavior; the asset-tree child owns expansion.
- Do not allow user `opener.edit` to bypass `yzn-open` in the managed sidebar.
- Do not create a separate repository.
- Do not change the packaged Yazi binary.

## Implementation Notes

1. Give the crate an argv-based internal CLI or another narrow interface that Nix can package without making it a public command.
2. Reuse the `toml` crate already accepted elsewhere in the repo rather than writing string-level TOML transforms.
3. Model only the small merge policy above. Treat unknown TOML nodes as opaque values owned by the user/Yazi.
4. Prefer explicit post-merge enforcement for `opener.edit` and the required Git fetchers.
5. Reuse current symlink behavior for immutable packaged files/assets where possible.
6. Delete the superseded materialization owner from `runtime/yzn-yazi.rs`; do not leave an adapter plus duplicate logic.
7. Keep tests strong and few: one representative merge contract, one invalid/collision failure contract, and preservation of the no-user fast path.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/yzn-yazi-config/Cargo.toml --all --check` should exit 0.
- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-yazi-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 and prove array replacement, integration-node preservation, invalid TOML diagnostics, and existing sidecar behavior.
- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 and prove the path-constrained Advanced action.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` should exit 0.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should exit 0.
- `git diff --check` should exit 0.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` should refresh the installed runtime.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if safe support requires validating arbitrary Yazi schema.
- Stop if more than the named integration nodes need custom merge semantics.
- Stop if the crate becomes an import/migration/package-management system.
- Stop if the existing init/keymap/plugin contracts cannot be moved without behavior drift; split the isomorphic move only if necessary to preserve one owner.

## Maintenance Notes

Effort: M. Risk: MED. Confidence: HIGH.

Rejected: generic deep-merge array concatenation, a whitelist of user-visible Yazi fields, string-level TOML editing, and a separate repository before the local contract is proven.
notes: Implemented one internal yzn-yazi-config Cargo owner. Native TOML tables merge recursively; scalars and arrays replace; plugin.prepend_fetchers uses replace-plus-managed-Git normalization; opener.edit is reasserted. Existing init, keymap, and plugin overlay behavior moved out of the launcher. Added Advanced yazi/yazi.toml action, Home Manager config.yazi.config passthrough, Ratconfig 59ced0e pin compatibility, docs, and LOC scorecard. Post-close simplification removed 26 tracked LOC from the first implementation, leaving net growth of 308 LOC (Rust +269, Markdown +18, Nix +14, TOML +7). Fresh-eyes fixed an over-complex Ratconfig test scaffold while retaining sibling-file isolation, and exercised the built materializer against real packaged and Home Manager TOML. Three crate contracts cover representative merging/integrations, invalid-or-collision failure preserving prior runtime, and packaged fast path. Final verification includes both Cargo suites and Clippy, focused Nix contracts, nix flake check, nix flake show --all-systems, nix build .#yzn, temporary profile install/status, git diff --check, and installed runtime refresh.
close_reason: Completed bounded native Yazi TOML materialization with one local Rust owner, launcher delegation, Ratconfig and Home Manager exposure, documentation, and full verification. Passed both focused Cargo suites, focused Nix contracts, nix flake check, nix flake show --all-systems, nix build .#yzn, temporary profile install/status, git diff --check, and installed runtime refresh.

title: Complete managed Yazi plugin and flavor tree
description:
## Why

The existing managed plugin overlay is useful but incomplete for a native power-user Yazi tree: it activates only when `init.lua` exists, has no flavor directory support, and ignores `package.toml`. Users should be able to keep plugins and flavors beside the managed native config without an import system or package manager.

## User-Visible Contract

The managed tree may contain:

```text
~/.config/yazelix-next/yazi/
  yazi.toml
  init.lua
  keymap.toml
  package.toml
  plugins/*.yazi/
  flavors/*.yazi/
```

Any supported input activates the generated runtime config home. Managed plugin and flavor directories become visible to Yazi, packaged entries remain intact, and name collisions fail clearly. Yazelix does not automatically copy from normal `~/.config/yazi`.

## Evidence

- `runtime/yzn-yazi.rs:32-39`: only init/keymap currently trigger runtime materialization.
- `runtime/yzn-yazi.rs:101-109`: packaged plugins are mirrored and user plugins are overlaid only when user init exists.
- `runtime/yzn-yazi.rs:135-170`: the current plugin overlay already has a small symlink and collision-rejection contract.
- `home-manager/module.nix:69-75`: only init/keymap files have declarative passthrough.
- https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/594 demonstrates the need to bring existing plugins into the managed tree.
- https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/358 demonstrates the need for current-format flavors.

## Scope

In scope:
- Move/extend the existing plugin overlay under the local materializer crate.
- Let plugins activate without requiring a user `init.lua`; users remain responsible for setup code where a plugin needs it.
- Overlay managed `flavors/*.yazi` directories using the same explicit collision policy as plugins.
- Pass through managed `package.toml` as opaque user/Yazi-owned metadata only if packaged Yazi consumes it from `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME`.
- Add a path-constrained Advanced action and Home Manager text/source passthrough for `package.toml` only if it is consumed at runtime.
- Keep directories user-managed; document copy/symlink workflows.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard.
- Refresh the installed runtime.

Out of scope:
- Do not run `ya pkg`.
- Do not add `yzn import yazi`.
- Do not read/copy `~/.config/yazi` automatically.
- Do not add plugin or flavor catalogs.
- Do not download, update, or validate third-party plugins/flavors.
- Do not let user assets replace Yazelix-owned packaged plugin names.
- Do not add directory-valued Home Manager abstractions unless a concrete declarative workflow cannot use existing xdg file ownership.
- Do not change `yazi.toml` merge semantics from the prerequisite bead.

## Implementation Notes

1. Generalize one filesystem overlay helper for plugins and flavors rather than duplicating traversal logic.
2. Define packaged-name collisions from the actual packaged directories, not a second handwritten list.
3. Preserve symlink-based low-churn behavior where Yazi accepts it.
4. Treat `package.toml` as opaque; do not parse or reconcile package dependencies.
5. Materialization should be deterministic and clean stale generated links without touching user inputs.
6. Keep one representative success test and one collision/stale-output failure test.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/yzn-yazi-config/Cargo.toml --all --check` should exit 0.
- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-yazi-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 and prove plugin/flavor visibility independent of init.lua, collision rejection, and stale-output cleanup.
- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 if a package.toml action is exposed.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` should exit 0.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should exit 0.
- `git diff --check` should exit 0.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` should refresh the installed runtime.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if this becomes a package manager or import/migration workflow.
- Stop if flavor support requires a catalog or rendering system.
- Stop if `package.toml` is not consumed at runtime; omit it rather than inventing ownership.
- Stop if collision handling grows beyond packaged-name rejection.

## Maintenance Notes

Effort: S-M. Risk: LOW-MED. Confidence: HIGH.

Rejected: main Yazelix import parity, automatic ambient config adoption, downloading packages, and a theme/flavor catalog.
notes: Implemented 2026-07-10. The local materializer treats plugins, flavors, package.toml, and theme.toml as independent managed inputs; one shared overlay helper retains packaged assets, links user *.yazi directories, rejects packaged-name collisions, and rematerialization drops stale links. package.toml remains opaque and Yazelix never runs ya pkg. Packaged Yazi 26.5.6 source inspection showed that flavors are selected only through YAZI_CONFIG_HOME/theme.toml, making opaque theme.toml passthrough the irreducible prerequisite for usable flavors. Ratconfig Advanced actions and Home Manager text/source passthrough expose both files. The Ratconfig flake pin advances to tagged v2.0.0 so Nix matches the Cargo lock and local development dependency. No test functions were added: the existing three materializer tests cover independent asset activation, packaged/user visibility, collision preservation, opaque files, and stale cleanup. Packaged yazi --debug exited 0 against a generated tree and reported theme.toml, package.toml, and example as both dark/light flavors while retaining the managed opener. A ruthless simplification pass removed hard-coded action counts and redundant literal path/test scaffolding, reducing the slice by 75 tracked lines without changing runtime behavior or test count. Fresh eyes then removed one remaining hard-coded Helix action split and confirmed Yazi package writes follow the managed symlink. Final tracked LOC delta: +71 (Rust +32, Nix +14, Markdown +25); lockfiles and Beads excluded. Verification passed before final publication: both Cargo fmt checks, both Cargo test suites (3 and 37 tests), both clippy all-targets with warnings denied, packaged Yazi debug loading, nix flake check, nix flake show --all-systems, nix build .#yzn, temporary profile add/list, installed profile refresh, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed managed plugin/flavor tree with native theme and package passthrough

title: Validate a power-user Yazi configuration end to end
description:
## Why

The new materializer is intended to solve concrete power-user workflows, not merely produce structurally plausible files. A final acceptance gate should exercise the exact failure classes reported against main Yazelix without adding permanent copied fixtures or broad compatibility tests.

## Evidence

- https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/355: user Lua/plugin setup must remain possible.
- https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/363: user tables and arrays-of-tables must survive materialization alongside packaged integration.
- https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/593: `mgr.ratio` must occur once and replace defaults rather than concatenate.
- https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/594: managed plugins must be visible without manual generated-state copying.
- https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/358: current-format managed flavors must load.
- `AGENTS.md`: prefer one strong contract test over several thin mirror tests and do not launch GUI sessions unless requested.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add one temporary-directory acceptance scenario representing a Soder-style power-user configuration:
  - `mgr.ratio = [1, 4, 0]`
  - `mgr.show_hidden = true`
  - preview limits
  - a user fetcher and previewer array-of-table
  - a synthetic user plugin required from managed `init.lua`
  - a synthetic managed flavor
- Materialize the runtime config and assert user values, packaged opener integration, required Git decoration integration, plugin/flavor visibility, and absence of duplicated ratio entries.
- Exercise the packaged Yazi's non-GUI config/debug parsing path when available so upstream schema drift fails during checks.
- Add a concise README power-user example based on the supported managed tree.
- Record any limitation that requires manual dogfooding, but do not claim GUI behavior from structural checks.
- Update LOC scorecard if tracked files change.

Out of scope:
- Do not vendor Yamb, Ayu, or other third-party fixtures.
- Do not contact an external reporter or require their personal configuration.
- Do not add a mirror test for every Yazi setting.
- Do not launch a GUI/interactive Yazi session automatically.
- Do not broaden the materializer during validation; open a focused follow-up only for a confirmed contract gap.
- Do not test automatic import from `~/.config/yazi`.

## Implementation Notes

1. Build the representative inputs inside a temporary directory at test time.
2. Prefer one end-to-end contract test over separate literal tests.
3. Use tiny synthetic plugin/flavor directories so the check is deterministic and license-neutral.
4. If packaged Yazi has no stable non-GUI config parse command, keep structural assertions and state that limitation rather than scripting an interactive session.
5. Treat any required merge-policy change as a return to the owning implementation bead, not validation-only logic.

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-yazi-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 and include the representative power-user scenario.
- The exact packaged-Yazi non-GUI parse/debug command selected during implementation should exit 0; record it in the bead notes and README development checks.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0.
- `git diff --check` should exit 0.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the implementation prerequisites are incomplete or their contract has drifted.
- Stop if validation requires vendoring third-party plugins/flavors.
- Stop if the only available verification requires an interactive GUI; report the remaining manual gap.
- Stop if the test begins mirroring the entire Yazi schema.

## Maintenance Notes

Effort: S. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.

This is an acceptance gate, not a new compatibility subsystem.
notes: Selected smart-enter.yazi as the real plugin fixture. The existing yzn_yazi_materialization check materializes the nixpkgs plugin with open_multi=false and an l binding, then packaged Yazi --debug validates the composed config and managed opener. Live dogfood config was installed under ~/.config/yazelix-next/yazi; an isolated interactive run confirmed l enters the hovered child directory via the recorded cwd-file. File opening remains delegated to the already validated managed opener.
close_reason: Validated a real Smart Enter power-user configuration through the managed materializer and packaged Yazi debug path.

title: Reassess Yazi materializer extraction with a second consumer
description:
## Why

The bounded Yazi materializer may eventually serve main Yazelix or another runtime, but extracting it before a second consumer would add release, pinning, API, and coordination overhead without deleting any real owner. Reassess only after the local crate's contract is stable and another consumer is ready to adopt it.

## Evidence

- `ARCHITECTURE.md:123-142`: separate repositories require independent users, release cadence, a stable artifact/API, low product coupling, and deletion of a real local owner.
- The local materializer implementation bead establishes a product-coupled config-home contract first.
- Main Yazelix currently owns a broader independent Yazi materialization surface; theoretical reuse is not adoption.

## Decision Criteria

Extract only if all are true:

- A concrete second consumer will call the same input/output contract.
- The second consumer deletes or relinquishes its existing materialization owner.
- The CLI/library contract is stable enough to version independently.
- Runtime-specific paths and policy can be provided without compatibility adapters or duplicate owners.
- Cross-repo release/pin updates are cheaper than atomic local changes.

Keep local if any are true:

- Yazelix Next remains the only caller.
- Main Yazelix still requires broader import/migration behavior.
- Extraction needs Next-specific path shims.
- Changes still need to land atomically with `yzn-yazi`.

## Scope

In scope when reactivated:
- Inspect all current callers and the local crate API/CLI.
- Identify the concrete second consumer and the owner it will delete.
- Compare local-crate versus shared-repo release costs.
- Decide keep local or extract.
- If extracting, create implementation beads with exact repository, artifact, API, and migration boundaries.
- Update ARCHITECTURE with the decision.

Out of scope:
- Do not extract code during this decision bead.
- Do not create a generic Yazi configuration framework.
- Do not add compatibility adapters for hypothetical consumers.
- Do not make extraction block power-user functionality.

## Verification

- `rg -n "yzn-yazi-config|yzn-yazi" .` and equivalent searches in the proposed consumer should identify every real caller.
- If no second consumer exists, close with a keep-local decision and no code changes.
- If extraction is selected, the follow-up plan must name the deleted owner and independent verification commands.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop immediately if no concrete second consumer exists.
- Stop if the proposed consumer cannot delete its current owner.
- Stop if the API remains coupled to Yazelix Next paths or atomic runtime changes.

## Maintenance Notes

Effort: S decision. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.

Current recommendation: keep the crate local. Rejected: extraction based only on possible future reuse.

title: Use TOML 1.1 in yzn-config
description: Upgrade yzn-config's existing direct toml dependency from 0.9 to the 1.1 release family as the Yazelix Next slice of main product-wide Bead yazelix-p1s7z. Preserve the current config model, parse/serialize behavior, and minimal ownership boundary; add no adapter or compatibility path.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- crates/yzn-config declares toml 1.1 and Cargo.lock resolves that direct dependency without a parallel first-party TOML family.
- Existing yzn-config behavior and runtime contracts pass their focused checks.
- README LOC scorecard remains accurate after the manifest/lock/Beads change.
- The change is committed and published for downstream product-wide evidence.
notes:
Dependency decision before implementation:
- Production crates: update the existing toml dependency to 1.1; add none.
- Dev-only crates: none.
- In-house logic: no new parsing or conversion logic unless the compiler identifies a required TOML 1.1 API adaptation.
- Rejected alternatives: keep 0.9, alias two TOML versions, or patch transitive third-party dependencies.
- Packaging impact: refresh Cargo.lock and run the focused Next contract/package checks.

Compile evidence before source adaptation:
- The isolated origin/main worktree resolves the intentional sibling path dependency to current published Ratconfig 59ced0e, which added section_label to ConfigUiField, ConfigUiFieldRowSpec, and ConfigUiStringListChoiceSpec after Next's last commit.
- Initialize those presentation-only fields to empty strings. This is the minimum compatibility update for the current child boundary, not TOML conversion logic or a new Next-owned feature.

Completion evidence:
- yzn-config now resolves one toml 1.1.2 package shared with published Ratconfig 59ced0e; the obsolete toml 0.9/winnow 0.7 lock entries were deleted.
- Next flake.lock now packages Ratconfig 59ced0e, aligning packaged and sibling-path development APIs.
- Added empty section_label presentation metadata to three existing Next row builders required by current Ratconfig; no behavior or new owner was added.
- Passed cargo fmt check, cargo check, and 38 yzn-config tests.
- Passed nix flake check, nix flake show --all-systems, and nix build .#yzn without overrides.
- After rebasing onto concurrent Next commits, README LOC scorecard is exact at Rust 11698 / total 14981.
close_reason: Completed: yzn-config and packaged Ratconfig use TOML 1.1; current Ratconfig section metadata is adapted; Cargo, all 38 focused tests, full flake checks, all-system evaluation, and yzn package build pass; LOC scorecard updated.

title: Add Yazelix Next light appearance mode
description:
## Why

Yazelix Next currently ships a dark-only curated runtime. `mars.toml` forces dark theme behavior, the Yazelix Zellij Bar render request uses `appearance_mode = "dark"`, and the rest of the packaged visual surface assumes dark colors. Users should eventually be able to choose a light appearance without editing every native tool config by hand.

## User-Visible Behavior

A user can select a Yazelix-owned appearance mode and launch the managed runtime with coherent light styling. Dark remains the default. Light mode should make the first viewport usable across Mars, Zellij bars, Helix/Yazi-visible surfaces where Yazelix owns the baseline, and cursor/color choices that depend on appearance.

Possible first semantic config shape, to be finalized before implementation:

```toml
[appearance]
mode = "dark" # dark | light
```

`auto` can be considered later, but the first slice should not require OS theme detection.

## Scope

In scope:
- Define the smallest light-mode contract before coding.
- Add a semantic config setting for appearance mode through the existing Yazelix config core.
- Keep `dark` as the default and preserve current runtime behavior when unset.
- Make `yzn launch`/`yzn enter` consume the setting consistently where the runtime already owns visual config.
- Update Mars packaged/materialized config for a readable light appearance.
- Pass the selected appearance mode to Yazelix Zellij Bar rendering instead of hardcoding `dark`.
- Decide whether Helix/Yazi defaults need light-specific packaged choices for the first slice; add only if the current dark defaults are visibly broken in light mode.
- If Yazelix cursors are integrated, let `yazelix-cursors` own appearance-sensitive cursor resolution rather than duplicating that logic here.
- Add focused checks for config default/validation and generated runtime fragments.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard.
- Refresh installed `yzn` after runtime flake changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not add broad theme packs.
- Do not add OS theme auto-detection in the first slice unless it is essentially free and checked.
- Do not port main Yazelix theme/materialization machinery wholesale.
- Do not add Ghostty packaging or terminal matrix support for light mode.
- Do not make Ratconfig own native theme semantics for Mars, Helix, Yazi, or Zellij.
- Do not add cursor registry logic here; keep reusable cursor behavior in `yazelix-cursors`.

## Implementation Notes

1. Start with one semantic field and one owner: `crates/yzn-config` validates the mode; runtime wiring consumes it.
2. Keep Mars as the terminal visual owner for the packaged app path.
3. The bar render request currently hardcodes `appearance_mode = "dark"`; this should become runtime/config-driven.
4. Keep visual validation mostly contract-based and manual-dogfood only where rendering cannot be checked cheaply.
5. Main Yazelix can be a reference for proven light-theme constants, but do not inherit its broad terminal/theme materialization system.

## Verification

- Config check proves missing `appearance.mode` defaults to `dark`.
- Config check rejects unsupported appearance values.
- Contract check proves the packaged/default runtime still emits current dark behavior.
- Contract check proves light mode changes the Mars/bar-owned fragments expected by the contract.
- `nix flake check`.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs`.
- Temp profile install with `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` if package surface changes.
- Manual dogfooding when requested: launch in light mode and verify Mars, Zellij bar, shell prompt, Yazi, and Helix are readable.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if this becomes a general theme engine.
- Stop if light mode requires broad native config merging or generated theme packs.
- Stop if the light experience cannot be made coherent in Mars without first fixing Mars itself.
- Stop if adding `auto` makes the slice depend on OS-specific detection policy.
notes: Completed with one semantic owner: yzn-config validates appearance.mode as dark|light and yzn runtime consumes it. Main Yazelix/Mars light colors were reused for the packaged light Mars config; the Zellij top bar is rendered through yazelix-zellij-bar with the selected appearance mode. Rejected alternatives for this slice: no auto mode, no broad theme engine, no Helix/Yazi light theme materialization, and no Ratconfig/native-theme ownership split.
close_reason: Implemented light appearance mode with config validation, runtime Mars/bar wiring, docs, changelog, scorecard, and contract coverage. Verified cargo tests, git diff check, nix flake check, flake show all systems, nix build, temp profile install, installed profile upgrade, and a non-GUI light-mode status/layout probe.

title: Add Home Manager yzn gate
description:
## Why

Home Manager is one of the documented install/config surfaces, and the main switch is close enough that we should test it with the actual `yzn` front door, not only with file-shape assertions. The existing flake check proves the module evaluates, installs a package, and renders config files, but it does not run installed `yzn` commands against the Home Manager-rendered config. A small HM-with-yzn gate catches the integration class where Home Manager output exists but the runtime cannot consume it.

## Evidence

- `README.md:81`: Home Manager users are documented as importing `inputs.yazelix-next.homeManagerModules.default`.
- `README.md:86`: the documented minimal setting is `programs.yazelix.enable = true`.
- `README.md:90`: `programs.yazelix.package` can override the installed package.
- `README.md:112`: `programs.yazelix.config.settings` renders `~/.config/yazelix-next/config.toml` with Yazelix defaults and Ratconfig contract state.
- `home-manager/module.nix:98`: the module exposes `programs.yazelix.package` and defaults it to the flake `yzn` package for the host system.
- `home-manager/module.nix:141`: enabling the module adds `cfg.package` to `home.packages`.
- `home-manager/module.nix:152`: the module renders `xdg.configFile` entries for semantic and native Yazelix config files.
- `flake.nix:537`: the current check builds Home Manager configurations through `home-manager.lib.homeManagerConfiguration`.
- `flake.nix:583`: the current `home_manager` check inspects `activationPackage` outputs.
- `flake.nix:588`: the current check verifies `home-path/bin/yzn` exists.
- `flake.nix:600`: the current check verifies rendered config text.
- `flake.nix:610`: the current check calls the packaged `yzn-config --get` helper, but it does not execute the installed `yzn` front door against the Home Manager result.
- `yazelix-next-version-bump-profile-gate-otg`: the version gate covers profile installs and installed non-GUI `yzn` commands, but not the Home Manager install/config path.

## User-Visible / Maintainer Behavior

A maintainer can run a Home Manager gate that builds a Home Manager configuration using the real `yzn` package, points installed `yzn` at the Home Manager-rendered Yazelix config files, and verifies non-GUI commands such as `yzn help`, `yzn status`, and `yzn doctor` succeed. Passing the gate means the documented Home Manager path produces a package/config combination that `yzn` can actually consume.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add a focused Home Manager + `yzn` gate after the baseline CI/version-gate workflow surface exists.
- Reuse the existing Home Manager module and `home-manager.lib.homeManagerConfiguration` pattern from `flake.nix`.
- Build a Home Manager configuration that enables `programs.yazelix` with the real package, plus a small semantic config override such as `shell.program = "fish"` and `welcome.enabled = false`.
- Use the resulting `activationPackage/home-path/bin/yzn` as the installed command under test.
- Use the resulting `activationPackage/home-files/.config/yazelix-next` as `YAZELIX_NEXT_CONFIG_HOME` when config files are enabled.
- Run installed non-GUI commands with isolated temp state/home variables: at minimum `yzn help`, `yzn status`, and `yzn doctor`; add `yzn tutor list` if the gate shares the version-gate smoke helper.
- Assert command output proves Home Manager-rendered config is active, for example `yzn status` showing the HM config home and configured shell.
- Keep this either as a dedicated job in the version-gate workflow or as a separate manual workflow if that is cleaner after `yazelix-next-a8g` lands.

Out of scope:
- Do not run `home-manager switch` against the maintainer's real home or a shared runner home.
- Do not mutate real user profiles, real `~/.config`, or real `~/.local/state`.
- Do not launch Mars, Zellij, Yazi, Helix, GUI sessions, or interactive `yzn config`.
- Do not broaden the Home Manager module surface or add new HM options in this bead.
- Do not make Home Manager own generated runtime state beyond the documented config files.
- Do not replace the existing fast `checks.home_manager`; this gate complements it with runtime command evidence.

## Implementation Notes

1. Implement `yazelix-next-a8g` first so CI setup exists, and prefer building on `yazelix-next-version-bump-profile-gate-otg` if that workflow already owns non-GUI installed-command smoke helpers.
2. Start with a non-mutating check using `home-manager.lib.homeManagerConfiguration` and `activationPackage/home-path`, because that is already what the flake check uses and avoids touching real profiles.
3. If a true `home-manager switch` style test is desired, only do it with a temp `HOME`, temp `XDG_*` dirs, and a temp profile after proving it cannot affect the runner/user profile. Otherwise record that as a rejected alternative.
4. Use temp environment variables when executing `yzn`: `HOME`, `YAZELIX_NEXT_CONFIG_HOME`, `YAZELIX_STATE_DIR`, and `XDG_DATA_HOME` should all point under the job temp dir or Nix-built HM config result.
5. Keep assertions behavior-oriented: the installed `yzn` should prepare runtime state and read the HM-rendered config, not merely have files in the right paths.
6. Avoid adding a local validator binary. Direct shell checks are sufficient until repeated gate logic justifies a helper.

## Verification

- `git diff --check` should exit 0 after workflow or check edits.
- `nix build .#checks.x86_64-linux.home_manager --no-link --print-build-logs` should still exit 0.
- The new HM gate should build the Home Manager activation package successfully.
- The new HM gate should execute the Home Manager-installed `yzn help` successfully.
- With `YAZELIX_NEXT_CONFIG_HOME` set to the HM-rendered config tree and isolated state dirs, the new HM gate should execute `yzn status` successfully and show the HM config path plus the configured shell.
- With the same isolated env, the new HM gate should execute `yzn doctor` successfully and print `Yazelix doctor`.
- If included, `yzn tutor list` from the HM-installed command should exit 0.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the implementation would run `home-manager switch` in a way that can mutate the real user or runner profile.
- Stop if the gate needs GUI sessions, Mars launch, Zellij server state, or interactive prompts.
- Stop if adding the gate requires broad Home Manager option redesign instead of testing the existing documented surface.
- Stop if the Home Manager activation output shape differs enough that the existing `activationPackage/home-path` pattern no longer proves installed command behavior; update the existing HM check first or record the blocker.
- Stop if this starts becoming release publishing or branch-protection policy work.

## Maintenance Notes

Recommended sequencing: baseline CI, version profile-install gate, then this Home Manager-with-yzn gate. Rejected alternative: only relying on `checks.home_manager`; it is still valuable as a fast flake check, but it does not prove the actual `yzn` front door can consume Home Manager-rendered config.
close_reason: Extended checks.home_manager to run the Home Manager-installed yzn against HM-rendered config with isolated state, covering help/status/doctor/tutor list.

title: Add guarded Zellij native config sidecar
description:
## Why

Zellij preferences should be user-editable, but a full native Zellij config replacement would let user config break the Yazelix Next runtime spine. The current packaged config owns the managed shell, key remaps, pane creation key, and sidebar toggle, so the first user config surface must be a guarded native sidecar for safe preferences only.

## Evidence

- `flake.nix:83`: the packaged Zellij config is generated from repo-owned `config.kdl` with the managed Nushell path substituted in.
- `flake.nix:187`: `yzn` launches Zellij with `--config ${yznConfigKdl}` and `--new-session-with-layout ${yznZellijLayout}/layout.kdl`.
- `config.kdl:1`: `default_shell` is managed and points at the packaged `yzn-nu` shell.
- `config.kdl:3`: `keybinds` is managed by Yazelix Next.
- `config.kdl:31`: `Alt m` is the managed new-pane binding used with the layout behavior.
- `config.kdl:32`: `Alt Shift h` is the managed sidebar swap binding.
- `layout.kdl:1`: layout ownership is separate from config and currently defines the managed tab/sidebar/status-bar structure.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:23`: `config.kdl`, `layout.kdl`, and `layout.swap.kdl` are documented Zellij behavior owners.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add an optional guarded sidecar at `~/.config/yazelix-next/zellij/config.kdl`.
- Build the runtime Zellij config from the packaged `config.kdl` plus the user sidecar only when the sidecar passes validation.
- Treat the sidecar as native Zellij preferences, not a full Zellij config replacement.
- Fail fast with a clear message when the sidecar contains dangerous top-level nodes or settings that would replace Yazelix-owned behavior.
- Add a focused contract check for accepted safe sidecar content and rejected dangerous content.
- Document the sidecar path, allowed intent, and forbidden ownership boundaries in `README.md` and `ARCHITECTURE.md`.
- Update `CHANGELOG.md` and the README LOC scorecard.
- Refresh the installed `yzn` runtime after changing the flake runtime.

Out of scope:
- Do not allow user replacement of `config.kdl`, `layout.kdl`, or `layout.swap.kdl`.
- Do not support arbitrary native Zellij keymaps in this sidecar.
- Do not support layout ownership, plugin alias ownership, default shell ownership, default layout ownership, session startup ownership, or pane policy ownership through this sidecar.
- Do not add semantic key remaps, profiles, ratconfig, Home Manager, or a CLI config editor in this slice.
- Do not silently ignore dangerous config. Reject it so the user knows why the sidecar is not being used.

## Implementation Notes

1. Define the allowed/forbidden sidecar contract before coding. Start strict and widen later only with concrete user value.
2. Prefer a small Rust validator if text checks would be brittle. The validator does not need a complete KDL semantic model; it only needs to reject forbidden top-level ownership nodes/settings reliably enough for the current contract.
3. Initial forbidden top-level items should include at least `keybinds`, `default_shell`, `default_layout`, `layout`, `plugins`, `load_plugins`, and any other node that clearly takes ownership of startup, layout, shell, plugin loading, or key policy.
4. Generate a runtime config under `YAZELIX_STATE_DIR` or another launch-local runtime directory by concatenating packaged config and validated sidecar content.
5. Launch Zellij with the generated runtime config path when a sidecar exists; otherwise keep using the packaged config path.
6. Keep layout and swap layout packaging unchanged.

## Verification

- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and include sidecar validation contract coverage.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0 and package the sidecar-aware launcher/config helper.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should exit 0 for a clean profile install.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` should refresh the user's installed runtime.
- Manual dogfooding, when requested, should show safe sidecar preferences applying without losing the managed shell, `Alt m`, `Alt Shift h`, sidebar layout, or bottom status-bar key hints.
- A sidecar containing `keybinds` or `default_shell` should fail before launching Zellij and print a clear diagnostic naming the forbidden node.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if Zellij sidecar support requires a full native Zellij config merge framework.
- Stop if the implementation cannot fail clearly on dangerous ownership nodes.
- Stop if supporting a requested preference requires touching managed layout, managed keymaps, or plugin orchestration; create a separate bead instead.
- Stop if the validator grows into a broad Zellij schema or semantic keybinding system.

## Maintenance Notes

- This sidecar is intentionally stricter than Mars config override because Zellij config owns the Yazelix Next runtime spine.
- Future semantic key remaps should be a separate owner-scoped config surface, not arbitrary native `keybinds` in this file.
- Rejected direction: letting `~/.config/zellij/config.kdl` or a full user-owned Zellij config become the default for managed `yzn` sessions.
close_reason: Implemented guarded Zellij native config sidecar: yzn validates optional config.kdl, appends safe preferences into runtime config, rejects dangerous ownership nodes, documents the surface, updates LOC, and passes flake/package checks.

title: Adopt Mars Yazelix config
description: Wire yzn to a package-owned Mars config root built from mars.toml, with Yazelix font settings, hidden window decorations, and reef split cursor colors.
acceptance_criteria:
yzn launches Mars with MARS_CONFIG_HOME pointing at a package-owned config root.
mars.toml owns the Mars settings, uses the Yazelix Mars font stack, disables window decorations, and hardcodes reef cursor colors using Mars-supported split cursor fields.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, and a temp profile install pass.
Installed yzn profile is refreshed.
notes: Implemented mars.toml as the yzn-owned Mars config. Verification: `nix flake show --all-systems` passed; `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` passed; TOML parse check confirmed confirm=true, bell audio=true, bell visual=true, font=JetBrains Mono, decorations=Disabled; built wrapper exports MARS_CONFIG_HOME=/nix/store/dqa1ldd7lnnwbdphf9ymhv3sa6nnb63z-yzn-mars-config; temp profile install exposed bin/yzn; `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` refreshed installed yzn to /nix/store/gp9l80zvz3skqzhn75wsylzs8x3r38b0-yzn. Leave open until human dogfooding confirms the Mars window looks right.

title: Add yzx screen command surface
description:
## Why

Main Yazelix exposes `yzx screen` as the integrated command for the standalone `yazelix-screen` animation engine. Yazelix Next should eventually support the same public surface so the canonical swap does not lose a familiar first-party command, while keeping the animation logic owned by the child repo.

## User-Visible Behavior

A user can run the integrated screen command from the Yazelix command surface:

```sh
yzx screen
yzx screen mandelbrot
yzx screen game_of_life_bloom --cell-style dotted
```

Before the public command swap, the implementation may expose the equivalent pre-swap command under `yzn screen`, but the final public contract is `yzx screen`.

No style means `random`, matching the standalone `yzs` behavior unless a deliberate Next-specific contract says otherwise.

## Source Context

- Child repo: `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-screen`.
- Child command: `yzs`.
- Child crate: `yazelix_screen`.
- Child contract: `yazelix_screen` owns reusable animation and terminal-rendering primitives; Yazelix product behavior stays outside the crate.
- Main Yazelix already consumes the child package for `yzx screen` and welcome/screen styles.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add `yazelix-screen` as a flake input/package dependency when implementing.
- Expose the integrated command surface, likely `yzn screen` before swap and `yzx screen` at public swap.
- Keep the animation/rendering engine in `yazelix-screen`; do not copy animation code into this repo.
- Forward supported style names and relevant options to `yzs` or a tiny local wrapper.
- Preserve the current `yzn help`, `config`, `enter`, and `launch` behavior.
- Add contract checks that the front-door help and command routing include screen support without launching a GUI session.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard.
- Refresh installed `yzn` after runtime flake changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not add a welcome screen in this bead.
- Do not add a Zellij screen saver in this bead.
- Do not port main Yazelix welcome-screen behavior.
- Do not add screen style settings to Ratconfig unless the user chooses that separately.
- Do not fork or duplicate `yazelix-screen` animation code.
- Do not require a full Yazelix managed session to run the screen command; it should work as a normal terminal command when possible.

## Implementation Notes

1. Prefer delegating to the packaged `yzs` binary if that keeps the slice small.
2. If a local wrapper is needed, keep it only for Yazelix command-surface behavior and argument validation.
3. Keep `yazelix-screen` as the source of truth for style names, frame rendering, terminal mode helpers, and cell-style parsing.
4. If the final public command name is not yet active, document the pre-swap `yzn screen` behavior clearly.
5. Main Yazelix can be used as reference for command shape, but do not port welcome-screen or screen-saver machinery.

## Verification

- Contract check proves help includes `screen` and preserves existing commands.
- Contract check proves `screen` routes to the packaged `yzs`/screen helper with forwarded arguments.
- `nix flake check`.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs`.
- Temp profile install with `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>`.
- Optional manual check when requested: run `yzn screen --help` and one bounded/noninteractive child check if available; avoid launching long animations in automated verification.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if this turns into welcome-screen or screen-saver integration.
- Stop if the child package does not expose a stable enough command/package contract.
- Stop if the command requires broad terminal capability detection before a useful first slice can land.
- Stop if supporting `yzx screen` before the public swap conflicts with the current `yzn` naming policy; use `yzn screen` until the swap.
notes: User expanded scope on 2026-06-30: implement the screen command plus startup welcome animation/static welcome screen. Add semantic settings to enable/disable welcome, choose style random or a specific style, and configure duration. Keep it simple: no configurable pool; random chooses one item from every yzx/yzn screen style plus the static welcome alternative. Current repo command remains pre-swap `yzn screen`; final public intent is `yzx screen`.
close_reason: Implemented yzn screen and startup welcome settings

title: Make yzn-open logging bounded or disableable
description:
yzn-open currently appends diagnostic events to `${YAZELIX_STATE_DIR}/logs/yzn-open.log` with no rotation or disable switch. This was useful for debugging Yazi-to-Helix opens, but it should not become an unbounded always-on log surface.

Acceptance criteria:
- Keep default diagnostics small and bounded, either with simple rotation/truncation or a clear opt-in debug mode.
- Provide a way to disable or minimize yzn-open logging without changing source code.
- Preserve enough failure evidence for Yazi open debugging when explicitly enabled.
- Keep the implementation simple and avoid introducing a logging framework unless the need becomes real.
close_reason: Implemented bounded yzn-open diagnostics: added YZN_OPEN_LOG levels off/error/info/debug, default info routing logs, debug-only payload/registry details, error-only failure logs, 64 KiB rotation with one yzn-open.log.1 backup, README/architecture docs, and tests for parsing, disable/error filtering, and rotation. Verified with cargo test, nix flake check, nix flake show, nix build, temp profile install, and nix profile upgrade.

title: Layer yzn Nushell user config
description: Allow yzn-scoped Nushell config layering without touching normal Nushell config. Packaged env/config should load first, then optional files under ~/.config/yazelix-next/nu via wrapper-generated runtime Nu source files.
close_reason: Implemented yzn-scoped Nushell config layering with packaged files first and optional user env/config files loaded from ~/.config/yazelix-next/nu via scripts/yzn-nu.sh.

title: Move screen welcome rendering into yazelix-screen
description: Move the bulky yzn screen/welcome renderer from yazelix-next into the sibling yazelix-screen package. Keep yzn-next as the owner of semantic welcome config, runtime env export, and command handoff only. Preserve yzn screen, welcome defaults, fixed random pool, static welcome card behavior, and duration setting. Main deletion target: remove crates/yzn-screen and consume yzs from the child package.
notes: Implemented locally across yazelix-screen and yazelix-next. Child repo now owns yzs screen style parsing, static/logo welcome card, fixed random pool, and --duration-seconds; yazelix-next consumes yzs and keeps only welcome config/runtime handoff. Fresh-eyes fixes: flake.nix/flake.lock are restored to the GitHub yazelixScreen input instead of an absolute local path; yazelix-screen README now states that standalone Yazelix-branded screen styles live in the child while integrated welcome/session policy stays in main, and its verification command matches AGENTS.md. Simplification pass: reduced local src/screen_runner.rs from 721 to 672 lines by splitting animation-only styles from static/logo styles, removing unreachable animation branches, collapsing duplicate timed/interactive animation loops, deleting one-call static card helpers, and replacing redundant Game of Life style validation with a direct list lookup. Follow-up simplification pass: removed the duplicate public SCREEN_STYLES list/export and prints help from SCREEN_RANDOM_STYLES plus random, keeping style order and help output intact; applied clippy-confirmed isomorphic simplifications in boids, kitty frame playback, and screen_runner. Current local screen_runner.rs is 661 lines. Rejected main-repo simplification for now: yzn runtime/config handoff remains explicit because it is the ownership boundary and only cosmetic line reductions were available. Until the child repo is committed/pushed and the lock is updated to that GitHub revision, validate main with --override-input yazelixScreen /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-screen. The installed yzn profile was refreshed with that override after the follow-up simplification pass. Do not close or publish main until the child repo is published and main validates without overrides per cross-repo release policy.
close_reason: Completed: moved welcome/screen rendering into yazelix-screen, pushed child revision 2cf5bbf, updated yazelix-next lock to that revision, validated without overrides, and kept yzn-next as config/runtime handoff owner.

title: Bridge shared TOML cursors into Ratconfig and Mars
description:
## Why

Yazelix Next currently launches Mars with a fixed cursor block and has no cursor configuration source in Ratconfig. The previous plan for launch-local Mars TOML materialization would add a second adapter even though Mars can consume the child crate directly. Cursor selection is cross-terminal product state and belongs in a separate `cursors.toml`, not `mars/config.toml`.

## User-Visible Contract

On first use, Yazelix Next initializes `~/.config/yazelix-next/cursors.toml` from the published child-owned TOML template without overwriting an existing file. Ratconfig has a Cursors tab for the common settings and opens the complete file for advanced custom definitions. A normal `yzn launch` passes that exact path to Mars. Named selections stay fixed; `random` resolves once per Mars process from the enabled pool with appearance-safe behavior. Changes apply on the next launch; no watcher or live per-window synchronization is promised.

## Evidence

- `flake.nix:4-50` consumes Mars but not `yazelix-cursors`.
- `runtime/yzn/cli.rs:122-146` starts Mars after `Runtime::prepare()`.
- `runtime/yzn/runtime.rs:114-121,219-242` currently passes only the selected Mars config home.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/model.rs:97-106` leaves cursor config paths empty and registers no cursor source.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs:698-705` explicitly keeps `yazelix.cursor.*` out of the Mars tab.
- `mars.toml:10-21` currently provides one fixed Mars cursor.
- Mars bead `yzt-8tg` owns the direct crate-consumption and native renderer adapter.

## Scope

In scope:
- Pin/package the published `yazelix-cursors` contract and a published Mars revision that completes `yzt-8tg`.
- Own the Next path `~/.config/yazelix-next/cursors.toml` and idempotently seed it from the child package.
- Add one Ratconfig source/tab for common cursor selection, enabled pool, effects, duration, glow, and supported fallback fields; obtain dynamic names and validation from the child crate rather than copying them.
- Keep advanced custom-definition editing as full-file open.
- Pass the config path to Mars through the explicit environment/config input defined by `yzt-8tg`.
- Remove the fixed cursor selection from the packaged Mars config if it would create precedence ambiguity.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and the LOC scorecard.
- Refresh the installed runtime after acceptance.

Out of scope:
- Do not parse or resolve cursor policy in `yzn`.
- Do not generate launch-local Mars TOML.
- Do not copy the registry into Ratconfig metadata.
- Do not add file watching, live reload, per-window control, Home Manager, or Ghostty config mutation.
- Do not implement the Mars renderer adapter in this repo.

## Implementation Notes

1. Complete the child TOML contract first and consume a published revision.
2. Complete Mars `yzt-8tg` and consume a published revision.
3. Treat Ratconfig as a view/editor: child types validate, while source metadata supplies labels and apply status.
4. Prefer one environment variable such as `YAZELIX_CURSOR_CONFIG`; do not add a wrapper command or generated config directory.
5. Dependency decision: use the child crate and existing TOML/Ratconfig stack. Add no random, schema, subprocess, or file-watcher dependency.
6. Keep one strong model/runtime contract check rather than separate literal/default mirror tests.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml --all --check` should exit 0.
- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml` should prove the Cursors source, dynamic allowed values, validation, and advanced-file target.
- Focused `runtime/yzn` tests should prove initialization is non-destructive and only Mars launch receives the explicit cursor path.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` should evaluate all current systems.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should succeed without local overrides.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should install a runnable package.
- Manual dogfood: select a fixed and random cursor through Ratconfig, open fresh Mars processes, and confirm unrelated Mars settings remain unchanged.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if implementation starts duplicating child definitions or random policy.
- Stop if Mars still requires concrete cursor fields in its own TOML; finish `yzt-8tg` instead of reviving materialization.
- Stop if Ratconfig cannot edit the TOML source without rewriting unrelated comments/custom definitions; keep common fields read-only until the child or Ratconfig owns a safe operation.

## Maintenance Notes

Estimated net Yazelix Next LOC: approximately 70 to 120, excluding lockfile churn. Rejected: cursor state in `mars/config.toml`, launch-local materialization, JSONC, and a `yzc` subprocess in the launch path.

title: Stop seeding Helix overrides from the packaged config
description:
## Why

The Helix runtime already deep-merges a user override over the packaged Nova config, but the Ratconfig file action creates a complete copy of that packaged config. Once a user opens the advanced file, untouched Helix settings are pinned and stop following Nova upgrades.

## Evidence

- `crates/yzn-config/src/catalog.rs:90`: `HELIX_CONFIG_STARTER` is the complete packaged `helix/config.toml`.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/file_actions.rs:48-57`: the Helix config action creates a missing user file from that starter.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/helix_config.rs:10-23`: runtime already deep-merges user TOML over the packaged config, so a sparse user document is sufficient.
- Other layered advanced files use small comments or minimal native documents rather than copied packaged state.

## User-Visible Contract

Explicitly opening `helix/config.toml` creates a minimal valid user override, not a snapshot of Nova defaults. User-written keys override packaged Helix configuration; untouched settings continue to follow upgrades. Existing user files remain untouched.

## Scope

In scope:
- Replace the full packaged starter with the smallest useful valid TOML starter, preferably a short ownership comment.
- Preserve the existing packaged-base plus user-overlay materializer and enforced reveal binding.
- Keep existing user Helix files unchanged; this unreleased path needs no compatibility rewrite.
- Add one focused contract check that a newly prepared override is sparse and packaged changes still flow through effective config.
- Update README or CHANGELOG only if current documentation or user-visible wording requires it, and update the LOC scorecard for project-file changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not add structured Helix fields to Ratconfig.
- Do not redesign Helix merge semantics, languages configuration, Steel files, keymaps, themes, or runtime state.
- Do not delete or rewrite an existing full user config.
- Do not add migration machinery or another crate.

## Implementation Notes

1. Use a comment-only TOML starter unless Helix requires a concrete table.
2. Retain the current explicit advanced action; unlike Mars, Starship, and Zellij, opening Ratconfig itself does not create this file.
3. Keep the effective config generated under runtime state by the existing materializer.
4. Prefer adapting one existing Helix materialization/file-action test over adding several literal tests.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml --all --check` should exit 0.
- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml` should prove preparing `helix/config.toml` creates only the sparse starter and effective config still merges an explicit user key over a changed packaged base.
- `nix flake check` and `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0 if packaged outputs change.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if Helix rejects an empty/comment-only TOML override.
- Stop if the runtime materializer no longer layers the user document over packaged config.
- Stop if implementation starts migrating existing user files or broadening Helix configuration scope.

## Maintenance Notes

- Effort: S. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.
- This is lower priority because file creation requires a deliberate advanced action.
- Rejected: keeping a convenient full starter at the cost of freezing every packaged setting; generating a second sample file.

title: Guard Zellij sidecar denylist parity
description:
## Why

The Zellij sidecar denylist is duplicated between the launch-time guard and the Ratconfig-side parser. If the two lists drift, `yzn config` could allow a sidecar entry that launch later rejects, or launch could reject something the UI thought was safe. This is a small but real ownership smell around safety-critical Zellij integration policy.

## Evidence

- `runtime/yzn-zellij-config.rs:8`: the launch-time sidecar guard declares `FORBIDDEN`.
- `runtime/yzn-zellij-config.rs:62`: launch validates each uncommented first token against that list before appending the sidecar.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs:107`: the config UI/parser declares a separate `ZELLIJ_FORBIDDEN_TOP_LEVEL` list.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs:1051`: Ratconfig-side parsing uses its own list when marking unsupported/blocked sidecar rows.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add the cheapest drift guard for these two denylist owners.
- Prefer a focused test or build-time check over a shared crate unless the surrounding code already moves toward a shared owner.
- Ensure the denied tokens remain the same from the user's perspective.
- Keep diagnostics readable in both paths.

Out of scope:
- Do not build a full KDL parser.
- Do not broaden sidecar support.
- Do not add a shared Rust crate only for this one list unless the implementation proves it is genuinely simpler.
- Do not weaken launch-time validation.

## Implementation Notes

1. First decide whether the cheapest check can compare literals from source files or packaged behavior without adding an abstraction.
2. If a source-level check is brittle, consider moving the list to one Rust module only if both binaries can consume it with minimal packaging changes.
3. Add a regression case that would fail if one path denied `default_shell` or `env` and the other did not.
4. Keep the list order stable unless tests only compare sets.

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 if config-side tests change.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and include the launch-side contract checks.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0 if helper packaging changes.
- `git diff --check` should exit 0.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the fix requires broad crate/package restructuring.
- Stop if a simple parity test would provide the same safety with less ownership.
- Stop if the current lists have already been replaced by a single owner; close this bead after verifying equivalent coverage.

## Maintenance Notes

This is a safety-policy drift guard, not a request to make Zellij native config more configurable. The sidecar stays intentionally narrow.
close_reason: Added zellij_sidecar_guard_parity flake check that extracts and diffs the launch-time yzn-zellij-config forbidden sidecar tokens against the yzn-config UI forbidden top-level tokens, with explicit default_shell/env regression coverage; nix flake check passes.

title: Launch the Yazelix Zellij fork under Mars
description:
## Context
Extend the tiny Yazelix Next launcher so `yzn` opens Mars running the Yazelix Zellij fork instead of a plain shell. The fork is the temporary Yazelix KGP/APC passthrough Zellij source consumed by main Yazelix, not upstream nixpkgs Zellij.

## Decisions
- Keep this in the flake/package surface only.
- Consume the Yazelix Zellij fork as a non-flake source input.
- Do not add Home Manager, layouts, config generation, plugins, or pane policy in this slice.
- Preserve the per-language LOC scorecard.

## Acceptance Criteria
See the structured acceptance criteria field on this bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- `flake.nix` has a `yazelixZellij` non-flake input pointing at the Yazelix fork branch.
- `packages.${system}.yazelix_zellij` packages that fork from source using nixpkgs' Zellij packaging as the base where possible.
- `yzn` launches Mars with the packaged Yazelix Zellij fork as its command.
- `nix run` and `nix run .#yzn` still use the same launcher app surface.
- README says `yzn` opens Mars running the Yazelix Zellij fork.
- README documents `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` as the installed-runtime update command.
- The per-language LOC scorecard is updated after the Nix and README changes.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` succeeds.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` succeeds or any build blocker is recorded with the exact failing package/dependency.
- A temp profile install using `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` exposes `<tmp>/bin/yzn`.
- The user's installed `yazelix-next` profile entry is refreshed after runtime changes.
- Human dogfooding confirms `yzn` opens a Mars window running the Yazelix Zellij fork before the bead is closed.
notes: Profile refresh evidence: ran `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next`; `nix profile list` now shows `yazelix-next` at `/nix/store/7imysps2q66y7aimv3xv6963hi6g3xhm-yzn`, and `/home/lucca/.nix-profile/bin/yzn` execs Mars with `/nix/store/3frryp6kl6sk7pqwcc0knksyf65411sa-zellij-0.44.3/bin/zellij`.
close_reason: Completed: yzn launches Mars with the packaged Yazelix Zellij fork; nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, zellij --version, profile refresh, and user dogfooding all passed.

title: Reject unsupported keys in the Nova root schema
description:
## Why

Nova currently reads the fields it knows but can leave unsupported or misspelled keys in `config.toml` without an actionable failure. At cutover that is dangerous: users can believe an explicit setting is active when the runtime ignored it. Nova must validate only its own final schema, strictly and generically, while remaining completely clean of legacy recognition or migration code.

## User Contract

- Every Nova `config.toml` key is either part of the current documented schema or causes a fail-fast error naming the unsupported path.
- Validation covers unknown top-level tables, unknown nested keys, wrong value shapes/types, and malformed custom popup entries.
- The dynamic `popups.<id>` namespace accepts arbitrary popup ids but only the documented fields within each entry.
- Sparse files remain valid: absence means inheritance from packaged defaults.
- Errors describe the current Nova schema and correction path. They never identify, infer, parse, translate, back up, move, or delete legacy configuration.

## Evidence

- `crates/yzn-config/src/root_config.rs` validates selected known values but currently starts from a TOML value that can retain unrelated keys.
- `runtime/yzn/runtime.rs` asks the config helper for known paths, so an unconsumed key can otherwise look harmless.
- Active executor `yazelix-next-8u5.3.1` owns the final field ledger and sparse persistence semantics; this bead begins only after that ledger settles.
- Main decision `yazelix-slim-runtime-boundaries-7ej8l.5.2` requires strict current-schema validation with zero legacy implementation in Nova.

## Scope

- Derive or define the allowed current-schema tree beside the existing catalog/validation owner; do not create a parallel schema catalog.
- Validate the entire sparse root before runtime use and before Ratconfig presents/saves it.
- Preserve canonical sparse overlay, explicit-equals-default intent, and arbitrary custom popup ids.
- Give one clear path-oriented diagnostic per invalid branch when practical.
- Update current-state README/architecture text, focused tests, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard.

## Rust Dependency Decision

- Production crates: retain existing `toml`, `serde_json`, and Ratconfig dependencies; existing UI crates remain unchanged.
- Dev-only crates: retain current test dependencies; add none.
- In-house: bounded traversal of the already-parsed current-schema value using the canonical field/custom-popup catalogs.
- Rejected: JSON Schema frameworks, a migration crate, a compatibility catalog, copied Classic schema, and a second parser.
- Packaging impact: none beyond rebuilding the existing config helper/runtime.

## Verification

- Focused tests reject one unknown top-level key, one unknown nested key, a misspelled known field, a wrong type, and an unsupported field inside an arbitrary custom popup id.
- Focused tests accept an empty file, representative sparse overrides, and multiple arbitrary popup ids with only supported fields.
- Runtime and Ratconfig fail on the same invalid file through the same validation owner.
- No test fixture, diagnostic, constant, or source identifier contains a legacy path or legacy field catalog.
- Run yzn-config format/tests, the packaged contracts check, `nix flake check`, `nix flake show --all-systems`, `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs`, and the LOC scorecard.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop until `yazelix-next-8u5.3.1` publishes and settles the final Nova field ledger.
- Stop if implementation adds a legacy filename, legacy field name, migration detector, backup/move action, compatibility alias, or full-default materialization.
- Stop if strictness requires duplicating the canonical field/custom-popup catalog instead of fixing its ownership seam.
- Stop if validation rejects valid sparse inheritance or dynamic popup ids.

Effort: M. Risk: HIGH. Confidence: HIGH after the field ledger settles. This is a Nova source bead and blocks the canonical cutover.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Dependency clarification 2026-07-12: this bead waits only for the Nova-only schema inventory from re-scoped 8u5.3.1. It does not wait for or consume the Main Classic loss budget, migration implementation, or legacy field catalog.

title: Publish the canonical Nova v1 Nix and Home Manager API
description:
## Why

The canonical swap must publish one small, explicit Nix interface rather than inheriting Classic aliases or exposing staging/internal child packages. Nova already has the right structural baseline; this task freezes the final names, adds the two missing install/declarative contracts, and deletes development-only product outputs at cutover.

## Canonical Nova v1 Contract

- `packages.<system>.default` and `packages.<system>.yazelix` are the same complete package and install `bin/yzx` plus package-owned desktop assets.
- `apps.<system>.default` and `apps.<system>.yazelix` run that package command.
- `homeManagerModules.default` is the only module output.
- Supported systems remain x86_64-linux, aarch64-linux, x86_64-darwin, and aarch64-darwin, with platform-specific desktop behavior gated intentionally.
- The flake advertises the Yazelix Cachix substituter/key and the source build remains a supported fallback.
- Checks remain maintainer/release evidence; their names are not promised product API.

The development repository keeps `yzn` names until the atomic canonical cutover. The cutover replaces them directly with `yzx`/`yazelix`; no `yzn`, `yazelix-next`, or compatibility aliases survive publicly.

## Home Manager Contract

Preserve Nova’s current small model:

- `programs.yazelix.enable`
- `programs.yazelix.package` as a whole-package override
- sparse `programs.yazelix.config.settings`
- native `text`/`source` options for Mars, Zellij, Starship, Helix, Yazi, and Nushell
- add `programs.yazelix.config.cursors.{text,source}` for the canonical child-owned `cursors.toml` after `yazelix-next-kdn` lands

Declaring no config writes no files. Presence of an option opts into Home Manager ownership. Do not add manage booleans, terminal selection, Mars-only package override, runtime tool source modes, component toggles, agent-usage package selection, per-field semantic options, or recursive packaged-default rendering.

## Removed Outputs

- Remove current staging product mirrors `packages.yazelix_helix` and `packages.yazelix_zellij`; they are integrated implementation inputs, not supported standalone Nova outputs.
- Do not add `lib.mkYazelix`, overlays, Classic runtime/package aliases, install-check packages/apps, child-package mirrors, or a public dev-tool package set.
- Standalone child users consume the owning child flake directly.

## Evidence

- Current `flake.nix` exposes only default/yzn plus two fork mirrors and one default Home Manager module.
- Current `home-manager/module.nix` already implements enable/package/sparse settings/native-file ownership without a builder.
- Main decision `yazelix-slim-runtime-boundaries-7ej8l.5.3` records the complete Classic loss budget and external-consumer audit.
- GitHub issue #658 proves representative Home Manager closures must be included in cache publication, not merely the product package.
- GitHub issue #649 proves cache absence must not make the source-build fallback invalid.
- Cursor executor `yazelix-next-kdn` owns the runtime/Ratconfig cursor path but deliberately leaves Home Manager for this public API task.

## Scope

- Apply canonical package/app/module names atomically with the public swap.
- Add Cachix flake configuration and ensure canonical cache workflow builds the product package plus representative Home Manager closure on supported release lanes.
- Add the cursor native-file option after the cursor contract is published.
- Remove staging fork mirrors and keep internal packages let-bound.
- Update Nova docs/checks for the exact public output and module contract; main cutover/release work owns Classic removal notes and the final canonical contract file.
- Run the Nova LOC scorecard.

## Dependency Decision

No Rust crate changes are expected. Reuse Nix/Home Manager primitives and the existing package/config owners. Add no package-builder framework, registry, overlay abstraction, or compatibility module. Packaging impact is smaller public output surface plus cache metadata; the cursor option installs only user-declared content.

## Verification

- `nix flake show --all-systems` reports exactly the approved product packages/apps/module and current checks.
